title: A Parallel in Time Algorithm Based on ParaExp for Optimal Control Problems
venue: In 2024 IEEE 63rd Conference on Decision and Control (CDC), 2024

abstract.en: We propose a new parallel-in-time algorithm for solving optimal control problems constrained by discretized partial differential equations. Our approach, which is based on a deeper understanding of ParaExp, considers an overlapping time-domain decomposition in which we combine the solution of homogeneous problems using exponential propagation with the local solutions of inhomogeneous problems.

title: Quantum approach for electrical system maintenance scheduling problem
authors: Q. Zhou, D. Tognon, S. Chalal, N. Henka, S. Tazi, F. Hafid, and C. Titouan

abstract.en: Maintenance scheduling is essential to ensure the reliability of power transmission systems while limiting operational risk and respecting technical and resource constraints.
abstract.fr: La planification des opérations de maintenance est essentielle pour garantir la fiabilité des réseaux de transport d'électricité.

title: A Dynamical Neural Galerkin Scheme for Filtering Problems
authors: J. Aghili, J. Atokple, M. Billaud-Friess, G. Garnier, O. Mula and D. Tognon

abstract.en: This paper considers the filtering problem which consists in reconstructing the state of a dynamical system with partial observations coming from sensor measurements.

title: Time parallelization and machine learning for optimal control and inverse problems
venue: Sorbonne Université, INRIA centre de Paris, France

abstract.en: PhD thesis on parallel-in-time methods for optimal control and a learning-based model correction approach inspired by Aphynity.
abstract.fr: Thèse de doctorat sur les méthodes parallèles en temps pour le contrôle optimal et une approche de correction de modèle par apprentissage inspirée d'Aphynity.

abstract.en: We present a convergence analysis of the ParaOpt algorithm applied to linear optimal control problems where the linear operator has at least one positive eigenvalue.

title: Convergence of ParaOpt for general Runge-Kutta time discretizations
authors: F. Kwok, J. Salomon and D. Tognon

abstract.en: We prove that the convergence rate of ParaOpt applied to a linear-quadratic optimal control problem has the same order as the Runge-Kutta time integration method used.
